data.title: Mecademic receives $21 million CAD (approx. $15.3 million USD) funding for global expansion from Investissement Québec and Export Development Canada.
data.description: Mecademic Inc., a prominent provider of compact, high-precision industrial robotics based in Montreal, recently secured a substantial investment of $21 million CAD (approximately $15.3 million USD). The funding round was spearheaded by Investissement Québec (IQ) and saw participation from Export Development Canada (EDC). This financial boost is set to drive Mecademic's innovative advancements in the robotics industry.

data.title: FTK Construction Services completes $13.6 million LIHTC renovation in Dallas, Texas.
data.description: FTK Construction Services recently finished renovating a property in Dallas, Texas. The project marks another successful completion for the company based in Allen, Texas. The completed renovation showcases the dedication and expertise of FTK Construction Services in delivering high-quality projects.

data.title: CobbleStone Software introduces Collaborative Online Editing for real-time contract teamwork, enhancing efficiency.
data.description: CobbleStone Software recently introduced a new Collaborative Online Editing feature aimed at enhancing team productivity and efficiency. This feature enables real-time contract drafting and negotiation. The announcement was made in Princeton, N.J., on February 20, 2026. CobbleStone Software, well-known for its contract management solutions, continues to innovate to cater to the ever-evolving needs of businesses. This initiative is expected to revolutionize the way teams work together, potentially speeding up processes and improving overall effectiveness in contract-related tasks.

data.title: Santa Barbara's UBGC appoints Melinda Cabrera as CEO, effective March 16, 2026, following an extensive search process.
data.description: In Santa Barbara, California, United Boys & Girls Clubs of Santa Barbara County (UBGC) has appointed Melinda Cabrera as their new Chief Executive Officer, starting on March 16, 2026. The selection came after a thorough search by the Board of Directors to find a leader with a successful background in the nonprofit sector. Prior to this role, Cabrera held the position of President and CEO at the Scholarship Foundation of Santa Barbara, which is known as the largest community in the country.

data.title: Western Union (NYSE: WU) stock down as Q4 earnings beat at 45 cents per share, sales at $1.008 billion (down 5%). Sales missed Street view at $1.045 billion.
data.description: Shares of Western Union Company (NYSE: WU) are down as the company released its fourth-quarter results. The company reported adjusted earnings per share of 45 cents, surpassing the analyst consensus of 43 cents. However, quarterly sales were reported at $1.008 billion, a 5% decrease from the previous year and below the expected $1.045 billion. The decline in revenue was primarily due to growth in the Consumer Services and Branded Digital sectors, counteracted by a slowdown in the Americas retail business. Despite efforts to enhance Consumer Services offerings and expand the retail footprint, the company faced challenges in the Americas market. The stock is experiencing notable weakness following the earnings report. Full details are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Comfort Systems USA's Q4 earnings beat expectations with EPS of $9.37 and revenue of $2.646B, driven by AI data center demand. Net income doubled to $330.8M from $145.9M. Revenue increased by 41.7% year over year, reaching $2.65B, with technology sector contributing 45% of total revenue in 2025. Backlog rose to $11.94B from $9.38B in Q3 and $5.99B a year earlier. Gross margin expanded to 25.5% from 23.2%. Operating cash flow reached $1.19B and free cash flow was $1.04B for the year.
data.description: Comfort Systems USA, Inc. (NYSE: FIX) experienced a rise in shares after reporting strong fourth-quarter earnings driven by increased demand related to AI-driven data center construction. The fourth-quarter results for 2025 included earnings per share of $9.37, surpassing the estimated $6.75, and revenue of $2.646 billion compared to the expected $2.337 billion. The technology sector contributed significantly to total revenue with a 45% share, a notable increase from the previous year's 33%. Net income more than doubled to $330.8 million from $145.9 million, and revenue saw a 41.7% year-over-year growth to $2.65 billion. Gross margin expanded to 25.5% from 23.2%, and adjusted EBITDA rose to $464 million, representing 17.5% of revenue. The full-year revenue amounted to $9.10 billion, up from $7.03 billion in 2024, and net income increased to $1.02 billion. The backlog reached $11.94 billion as of December 31, 2025, showing substantial growth from $9.38 billion in September and $5.99 billion the previous year. The company's operating cash flow was $1.19 billion, and free cash flow stood at $1.04 billion for the year.

data.title: MediaAlpha (MAX) set to report quarterly earnings on 2026-02-23. Analysts expect EPS of $0.34. Investors anticipate the results eagerly.
data.description: MediaAlpha (MAX) is set to announce its quarterly earnings on Monday, February 23, 2026. Analysts predict an EPS of $0.34 for the company. Investors are looking forward to the report with hopes of surpassing expectations and receiving positive guidance for the upcoming quarter. Stock prices can be greatly impacted by future forecasts rather than historical performance. In the last quarter, MediaAlpha fell short of EPS estimates by $0.05, highlighting the importance of closely monitoring company reports for investment decisions. For further details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Harvey AI boosts productivity by 35%, saving 7-10 hours per lawyer weekly. Legal tech firm eyes $11B valuation with $200M fundraising.
data.description: Case studies on the use of Harvey AI have demonstrated a weekly savings of 7-10 hours per lawyer. The legal AI startup is currently in the process of raising $200 million at a valuation of $11 billion, following the achievement of $190 million in annual recurring revenue. For further details, refer to the full article.
